reviews.review_description:
This concept is quite original and pretty interesting to me. I had more effective jump scares in this game by simple geometry than I have had from bloody and gorey threats from monsters in other games. The atmosphere hit the mark for me. I was in a place buried 
 deep inside my mind that should be a safe space sheilded by walls but it was not and the squares took on a whole new representation of my fears. I like that the puzzles were not guided and I had to sit and ponder a moment what to do to move on. Looking forward to more levels.

I agree with all the positive reviews here. Definitely worth trying even in early access. I've played both levels, really enjoy them but gosh that maze at the end of level 2 doesn't want to let me pass. Has me going in circles...er I mean squares! 

I've mainly played the 'escape the grid' mode and it has a nice variety of abstract rooms to explore. Some are purely atmospheric, others have an 'enemy' or environmental challenge to navigate. Would like to see more rooms in the future and feel like there are almost unlimited possibilities for level design and game modes so hope the dev keeps adding more content. I actually really like the music and sound design, but it might be cool to have an option to switch off music so that players can explore these environments with their own tunes on. Smooth turn would also be good, but snap isn't really affecting gameplay for me.

Would recommend to anyone looking for a liminal space / spooky / sci fi themed experience. Would also recommend to those new to vr as it has very impressive visuals with a great sense of scale and depth.

reviews.review_title: Clever game with some technical issues
reviews.review_description:
If you're looking for something truly unique and unsettling and don't mind Early Access I highly recommend picking this one up.

I really love the look of this game.  The art direction is beautifully grim and the world is very compelling.

The sound effects and music are great.

There are occasional puzzles and some nerve wracking platforming. I haven't finished the second level yet, but the variety of situations is already interesting. The developer has come up with some very clever scenarios.

And despite years of playing VR horror games and becoming slightly desensitised to all but the scariest ones, I do get pretty unnerved playing this.

I'm scared by squares.

There's a slight performance issue and the game occasionally hitches on the Quest 3. The hitching seems to be becoming more frequent as the game progresses.

There's also a strange bug where the environment can shift forward or backwards sometimes while turning. I play standing using natural turning. It's possible the game has only been tested with artificial turning.

There's a design choice that detracts from immersion. When walking any change in elevation results in an instant jump in your vertical position which is jarring and distracting. When walking across uneven surfaces you jiggle up and down. I wish this instant position change could be smoothed out.

The game is in early access so hopefully the technical issues can be sorted out.

I honestly think this game is a gem in the making, it has all the aspects of what a horror vr experience should be. I know what im about to ask is probaly the samething everyone asks buuuuuuuuut could we get monthly updates or a intense fear game mode.
All best wishes, from me

Ps, could you make the flash light slightly bigget on the hand cuz it looks goofy as a small dot on the hand

This game is insane. It scared the crap out of me so many times (still haven’t finished it). Finally getting to the colorful part felt so rewarding. But at the same time, that’s when it gets even scarier, which was genius. The whole thing feels like a “good, comfy nightmare” — but yeah… I’m a crazy person. I’ll follow this developer to the end of the world. Bravo!

This title has a very unique atmosphere that I have never experienced in a game before. Always has you trying new and different things to complete each level. The developers are adding new modes which add additional playablity to an already eerie game. Great work and looking forward to all the new modes and levels you have planned for the final version of the game!

What a unique and original idea.

I entered thinking it’s just squares, but woah it’s tense lol 
The atmosphere in this is great! The style and immersion is so cool! Sound so haunting!
Being chased and haunted by squares… panic and tension… arghhh they are after me, get me out of here!!!!

You advance through puzzles on each floor, whilst being hunted, chased and pressured by the square!

It really is a unique experience what they have here so far… with more to come, as this is early access… but looking forward to where they go with this one.

If you are looking for a unique, tense experience… it’s worth grabbing this one.
Dev gave up. Not worth to buy in current state! Only 1.5 lvl. 30min gameplay.

I can't believe this game's already been out for 4 months and the developer hasn't even added a smooth turn option for veteran VR players. I know Snap turn helps some people with motion sickness but where I've been playing VR for 9 years it totally breaks the immersion for me. I emailed the developer asking about adding smooth turn options with no response. It's a good game that's why I gave it three stars instead of one. It could be a great game with the right player options. If and when the developer adds more options for the player I will change my review.

developer_response.body:
Hey Rexwon,

You’re right, smooth turning has been requested quite a bit and it is planned to be added in an upcoming update.

I’m a solo developer, so features like this can take some time to implement properly. Since the game is in Early Access, I’ve been focusing on stability and game breaking issues first. Expanding player options is important though, and smooth turning is coming.

I really appreciate the feedback and your patience while I continue improving the game.

reviews.review_description: Didn’t expect this to be as creepy as it is. Super simple idea but it works really well in VR. Runs great on Quest 3, looks clean, and the sound design keeps you on edge the whole time. Easy to pick up, genuinely tense, and doesn’t rely on cheap jump scares.  the game play mechanics are simple to learn but smartly designed, keeping things engaging as it becomes more difficult. If you enjoy tense, minimalist horror or unique VR ideas, this one is absolutely worth your time.

reviews.review_title: I AM COMPLETELY SCARED BY SQUARES
reviews.review_description:
You wonder how a cube can scare the life out of you, but believe me, every corner, every cube has a hidden scary feeling, played through the campaign and level 2 certainly raised the difficulty and scare factor.
Some difficult invisible section proved very challenging for my memory bank.
This certainly is a game like no other on Quest  and definitely worth your time if you like an eerie feeling game.
Just buy it.

Given the game for free. Truth is if you want a challenge, if you are mentally APT, this is a genuine game for YOU. IF you give up quick and are mentally not up for it, don't bother! IF we are being honest here.. and you're up to it! Right? So give it a go, you are smart, and are willing to go on an adventure go on and, give it a go, go all out! This is for you!

It's actually really good. I got jump scared by a cube 🤣. Only one and a half levels. Got a bit repetitive towards the end. Glad for the wait on the next bit to have a break. Hopefully there is a story with the boys voice. 
Worth getting, a simple idea very well executed.

Huge thank you for replying to my last review. Sorry to have been so negative about the lack of save points.

I can only guess at how frustrating it must be  not being able to fully realise your own games vision  if it refuses to technically “play-ball”.

You are right , we can’t all play full-on for an entire session , especially something so immersive and deep as your incredible cuboid world. Save / check points are fundamental life savers for progress and enjoyment overall.

I shall “park” my disappointment and wait for everything to be finished and working. To everyone else reading , this is a remarkable game so far and the scale and scope of its ambition is already evident. It’s a strange , alien landscape that suits VR perfectly and offers just the right level of scariness and challenge.

I hope it works out and becomes the final game it should be. 

To other developers reading this , hopefully these early technical challenges and glitches become fewer and fewer in your games too.

Hey ho !

Cheers.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a ton for taking the time to write this and for the kind words you shared earlier. That honestly means a lot.

You did not miss a save point. I had to temporarily remove saving because of a game breaking bug it was causing. I know how frustrating that is, especially in a bigger area like Floor 2, and I am really sorry you ran into that.

Saving and checkpoints are one of the top things I am working on right now and they will be coming back as soon as it is safe to do so. I completely agree that being able to stop and come back later is super important, especially when you cannot play in one long session.

Thanks again for the support and for calling this out. Feedback like this genuinely helps me make the game better as I keep working on it.

reviews.review_title: Great game but no check points
reviews.review_description:
Until the developers add checkpoints I’ll change it to 5 stars. 

It’s an amazing puzzle game. With horror and beauty. But needs check points

developer_response.body:
Hey, I totally agree with you on the checkpoints. They were actually in the game, but I ran into a pretty nasty bug that was breaking saves, so I had to pull them for now.

Trust me, I want them back in just as much as you do. I’m working on fixing the issue so I can re add them in an update soon.

Really appreciate you playing and leaving feedback. It honestly helps a ton.

reviews.review_description:
So I've had the quest for a couple years now.... I've had to wipe my larger capacity oculus 3 a few times I have so many games......I love scary games and this one deff had it's jump scares....the developer got me so excited I decided to make a review for my first time so here u go......

First off I don't know pixels or what shaders are or nothin too technical but I know this 

It looks amazing.... I mean you're dealing with squares so it's not that complex but the artwork is very trippy. 
..... For Thursday you like to jump this game will definitely do it...... And it comes when you least expect it.......

So there were two floors with one floor in development that you cannot access.....

It was released not too long ago.... But already they dropped a huge update and added an entire new floor that has 25 rooms they each room has its own challenge..
.
What!

That was such an awesome surprise....... The only things different about this game I would maybe like to see is the option to run!

Thank you guys for creating an awesome game and thank you for keep working on it to make it better 

Definitely buy this game you won't be upset 
Zack

I've tried a few "Indie" VR games like The Hole Never Ends and Deadly Delivery and this is a cut above the rest.  The atmosphere and sound design make this an uneasy, thrilling experience.  From what I've played so far - it's more a liminal experience akin to "Pools" rather than jump scare horror.  The only problem is the lack of save points for us folks that can't complete it in one session but it looks like the dev is hard at work fixing that.

Like… HOLY SQUARE! Dude this game is great. I wasn’t too invested in the first floor but than… Dude the beginning of floor 2… OH MY! Dude that is sooooo Good! Like I’m not gonna spoil anything but oh my gosh… YOU GOTTA TRY IT! Just like. WOW! You GOT to try this game. Anyway… Thanks for reading my review I’m gonna go n- OH MY IS THAT A CUBE!!! HELLLLLLLLLLPPPPPPPPP AGHHHHGHG- 🔲

reviews.review_title: I found out you get scared by squares
reviews.review_description: Scary cube square man
I never thought squares could be this creepy. Fantastic game and it’s still got more levels to come. Well done guys.

OK...first off, I love this game.  The only gripe I have is that I can't save my progress.  Outside of this "gripe", it's perfect.  But since that "gripe" exists, it's because the game lacks what would EASILY make this game perfect.  
People, like me, may not have the time to play completely through one of the two provided levels of this game...so "Save Points" would be a wonderful option to have...but that scenario doesn't currently exist within this game.  If "Save Points" did exist, I'd change my 3-star rating to a 5-star INSTANTLY.  
So why not implement Save-Points?  
It's easy to do.  So why not do it?  
PLEASE, DEVELOPER, understand this.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review. You’re totally right about saving. There’s actually a save system in the game already, but it’s buggy right now and not working the way it should. It’s on my big list of fixes I’m working through as a solo dev, and I expect to have it working soon.

Really appreciate you taking the time to share this. I hope I can earn that five star once the fix rolls out.

reviews.review_description: Challenging. On Floor 2 but does not save progress. Any reason why.

developer_response.body: Hello! Unfortunately there was a bug with the save system, so I had to temporarily remove it from the game. It’s a top priority and should be fixed and back in soon.

reviews.review_title: Game is spectacular but not as scary.
reviews.review_description:
Not as scary as people make it out to be. Sure it’s ominous, surreal and totally creepy.

The scariest game I played would be Dreadhalls, so scary in fact, I couldn’t finish it. 

The scariest game I actually finished would be Propagation. 

So you can make an estimation there.

This game is what I always look for in a VR game. Original. This is such a wild idea that you’re surprised there’s even a story. The story line makes a lot of sense and really gives you a reason to proceed. 

The creep factor is so entertaining. Squares going in and out on your periphery. Peek a booinging you and jump scaring you. Landing from above. 

I personally prefer creepy horror games that don’t always put you on edge. This is a comfortable experience for me. Gives me more time and nerves to actually enjoy the grandiose surroundings. This is certainly a smart game that’s loads of fun.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the thoughtful review! I really appreciate you taking the time to write all that out. Totally fair take on the scare factor too. Scared by Squares leans more into that uneasy, surreal kind of tension rather than pure terror like Dreadhalls or Propagation.

I’m really glad you enjoyed the atmosphere and story side of it though. That balance between creepy and playable was something I really wanted to get right. Hearing that it gave you room to explore and take it in means a lot. Thanks again for playing and for such a great write-up!

reviews.review_description:
I really didn’t know what to expect. But literally everything is made with cubes (squares). There are odd sounds and voices, but the jump scares took the life out of me. 

The best concept of this game is that there are no scary images. I can be content to let my kids play it without them complaining about having nightmares of demonic images. Good on you developers!

I still give the game 5 stars, but there is one thing I wish would change. If you die from a cube touching you, I’d like to see the cube respawn and still be able to kill you again after you respawn. I’ve seen a scenario like that on floor 1 where a square killed me but when I respawned, it didn’t come back and I ran straight thru the puzzle without a challenge.

I’m excited for more floors! Keep them coming…

reviews.review_title: The Essence of Punking Set to 3D Graphix
reviews.review_description: This game has the optics and atmosphere of a psychological thriller, but at its heart it is a maze platformer that makes you occasionally scream and feel silly for having a jolt as it employs a level design that makes the world feel ominously alive and that it's actively responding to (and messing with) you. There's an underlying sense of humor peaking through it all; I even laughed out loud on one part where it pranked my expectations. A definite recommend.

developer_response.body: This might be one of the best summaries of the game we’ve seen. Thank you for sharing such a thoughtful review and for recommending the game!

reviews.review_description: I know this game is still in early access, but I just beat Floor 1 and honestly, I was expecting more than what I paid for. It has the concept of becoming a decent psychological horror exploration game with a couple of puzzles and a few "jumpsquares", just change the title cause let's be honest, having games rely on jump scares, doesn't make them scary. If anything, the music also breaks the immersion. Now it being dead quiet and squares pop up with loud noises? Okay. You might get spooked there.

Right now this is in Early Access, and it's only a few bucks.  Totally worth it.  I'll start by saying that I don't normally love horror games, especially if they involve relentless menacing zombies coming after you.  But more quiet, subtle psychological horror games can be fun.  This is more of that type. 

No monsters.  No blood and gore.  Just squares.  And yet the sense of brooding imminent peril is well-developed, and is really helped along by the soundtrack.  The puzzles are fun, intuitive, and not too tricky at all.  I will also thank the developer, because although turning motion movement is not yet smooth (I understand the developer is working on that), but the physical movement is quite forgiving.  You don't die easily, just because you didn't jump to *exactly* the right place. I love that.  I'm not a platform-type gamer at all (I like puzzle games and escape rooms, mostly) so I get quickly frustrated with any game where I need to be precise in moving, jumping or shooting.  Dying a thousand deaths is never fun.   So I really like that this is not a required element to enjoy this game.  Good work,  developer.  This is really fun so far. You're on the right track!

An intriguing and fresh concept, the use of so many primitive shapes means environments can be very expansive and visually more interesting than most Quest games. Which is a great thing, as you'll spend much of your time wandering the maze of cubes, battling periodic jump scares interspersed with moments of more interesting mechanical, platforming gameplay. Reminds me a lot of the vibe given by Squid Games, which has that mix of whimsical shapes, colors and music nicely counterplaying against the terrors.  Like an amusement park gone wrong. I like it.  Game could desperately use a few quality of life improvements though before leaving early access.  Perhaps I missed it but there seems to be no way to save and resume? Though not especially long, each level must be completed in a single session or you'll have to restart as I did. Also the game bizarrely doesn't support roomscale movement, so unless you're perfectly still it will continuously teleport you back into place, resulting in an annoyingly jumpy experience in an otherwise performant game.

To be immersed in a game that feels alive is a feat in itself, but to also feel alive within that atmosphere is genius. You genuinely feel connected to the game in a deep way as if the squares know you and are intentionally drawing upon who you are to create a pathological and psychological journey.

There is nothing like this…truly…and the experience will stretch and stay with you long after you put down the headset.

The game is a form of art that takes you places only nightmares can.

Fantastic.
Haven't beaten it yet but the first hour is very impressive. The sense of scale is incredible, and it's genuinely unnerving. Excellent work!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the great review! Super happy you are enjoying the game so far :)

reviews.review_description:
After winning a key for this game was excited to play it only the 1st floor took a bit of figuring out but once I got the hang of it off into the darkness I went.

Straight away the scare factor kicked in I was constantly on edge the layout is like no other game ive played and very well done.

Many of you will think as I did how can squares scare you I urge you to give it a go and see for yourselves.

I look forward to see where this game goes with upcoming updates and will definitely keep playing.

DONT BE A SQUARE AND GIVE IT A GO.

reviews.review_title: Maze killed the game for me
reviews.review_description: Concept and atmosphere are really great.  However, the f***** maze just made me lose my patience so much…tried like 20 times and gave up.  Refunded.  It’s too bad because I really liked the game so far.  Please consider making this easier, optional, I don’t know.  Will probably buy again if this is addressed.

developer_response.body: I’m sorry to hear you didn’t enjoy the game. If you ever decide to give it another try, you’re always welcome to join our community on Discord for help with any challenges you run into. The community is very active and often quick to offer guidance, especially for tricky areas like the maze. I also stop by occasionally to lend a hand myself. Player feedback in the Discord has a huge impact on future updates and changes to the game, so your input really helps shape how it improves over time. There are also full playthroughs on YouTube that I’d suggest checking out if you get stuck, they can really help with navigation and puzzle sections. Hopefully it can be a better experience for you in the future!

reviews.review_description: This is exactly why we need to support solo devs. This was such a fresh take on a game and a must play on the Quest if you're looking for something new and unique. Great ideas throughout. It's still early days, but I felt like I got my monies worth for sure. I'd love to see collectibles hidden in the nooks and crannies to offer replayability - maybe they offer some little story bits too to flesh out the world. I highly recommend this game. At least try out the demo.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the great review and your support in general! We have a ton of ideas for expanding the game moving forward. I think some will align with what you are wanting to see!

reviews.review_description: Play The Level. One in my opinion is the best. I got jump, scared by cube. Very fun. I would recommend in my opinion.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the great review! Thank you for recommending the game. Your support means a lot!

reviews.review_description: I can play this game for hours … I can also die a lot during that time :D.  The mazes are my favorite part - most of it is intuitive as to where you need to go, but getting there can be the hard part. I like the psych-thriller type music and have jumped a couple of times because of a square. The joy for me is in the simplicity of the game - but that does NOT mean it is easy. It makes you think, but not in really complex ways. It’s engaging and challenging, but do-able. Right now I’m lost in a maze on the second floor, but am looking forward to getting back in the game to figure it out. This is exactly the type of game I was looking for, thank you!

developer_response.body: Hey there, thank you very much for the detailed review and for sharing your experiences! Hopefully, you will be able to solve the puzzle and continue your journey. 👀

reviews.review_description:
not like that I fell over but those cube jumpscares in the first grey level are insane 💀 I thought how this game possibly could be scary and what the jumpscares could possibly be with only cubes, well, now I hate cubes 😭 freaked me out like crazy, this is the most I ever jumped in a vr game. its fun though 😂, love the atmosphere even though the graphics could maybeee receive some more polishing. Like a bit of reflections, light stuff etc. but still is awesome.
Currently at the 2nd rainbow cube level :)

also the jumpscares hit very unexpectedly 💔 when I was ready for it nothing happened but then out of nowhere I get jumped by those freaky cubes in the most random location (except the sneaky hallways where theres a corner to turn around back to back...)

reviews.review_title: Who Knew Squares Could Be This Scary?
reviews.review_description:
This is one of those games that sneaks up on you—in the best way. It looks simple at first, but don’t be fooled: it’s clever, tense, and seriously addictive. 😄

The mix of puzzle-solving and creeping dread works brilliantly. Every level makes you think, plan, and then panic slightly when things don’t go quite to plan. The atmosphere is spot on for VR—subtle scares, jump scares, smart use of space, and that constant “just one more try” feeling.

Controls are intuitive, performance is smooth, and the game does a great job of keeping you engaged without relying on cheap jump scares. It’s more about tension, timing, and your own nerves… which somehow makes it even scarier.
A smart, original, and surprisingly intense experience. definately one for the VR library.

Phenomenal game! Highly recommend, especially if you're into atmospheric horror games. 

Who knew squares could be so scary. 😁

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words, more coming soon! ⬛

reviews.review_description: I’ve only played the first level, and the level design is awesome! It’s dark, scary atmosphere and most importantly out of cubes! I don’t really have anything to complain about, i indeed did get scared by squares;)

reviews.review_title: A Must have Game on the Quest 3
reviews.review_description:
Check out my Review and Footage Videos on YT under Gotcha VR.
It's just brilliant. you wouldn't think a game like this would be so thrilling with it being based on Squares! But it has the Vibe, the atmosphere, the sound track, the puzzles.
I want More! It's just so good!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! That means a lot to hear. I really appreciate you sharing it on YouTube too, I’ll definitely check out your video. It’s awesome knowing the atmosphere and puzzles landed well, especially with something built entirely around squares lol. I’m working on more content, so hearing you want more is the best motivation possible!

reviews.review_title: A tough little puzzler with scares
reviews.review_description: Squares made me need new underwear. Really love the art direction of this.

developer_response.body:
They do stuff like that from time to time.

Thank you! :D

reviews.review_description: It's a great game, very creepy and surreal. I would purchase it

developer_response.body: Thanks friend, happy to see that you had fun!

reviews.review_description:
This game is as subtle as they come , 
Minimalist graphics, mixed with great audio makes this a great game , 

Greats blacks and whites work perfectly

developer_response.body: Super Happy to hear you are enjoying the vibe of the  game! :)

reviews.review_title: Payed the demo and am excited to play
reviews.review_description: Howevetr when I boot the game I hear piano music and all I see is darkness.  Button presses dont seem to do anything.l

developer_response.body: Hello! If you are using a program that changes the quest resolution that will mess things up. Turn that off and it should work fine. If you are still having issues join the discord linked at www.scaredbysquares.com and we can help you out.

reviews.review_description: So far, this game was really fun. Of course it was a bit short as it's in early access, but I had a blast playing it. The ambience was amazing and the puzzles were challenging while also simple at the same time so someone like me (a little slow) would understand it quick. I love the little lore going on too, and hope further on it'll be explored with more as we get to see what happened/happens with the daughter since clearly something happened. Overall super enjoyable, and it was a unique and different experience I've never seen in games before. Bizarre (in a good way) and incredible. I hope this game sees a full, complete story soon and I will definitely love to play more of it

developer_response.body:
Thank you FaithfulWish! More content is coming soon for sure, early access launch means the game is not done yet and we keep developing it, adding new features & levels.

Thanks a lot for the support!

reviews.review_description:
It would seem just cubes/squares, but I almost immediately immerse myself in this atmosphere of corners and fear
small labyrinths and puzzles on reaction and time activate the brains to try to get out as soon as possible... I'm looking forward to new levels

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We are already working on the new updates and levels. 😅

reviews.review_description: Such an orangional and creative idea! And yes I did get scared at one point. I just started it and the puzzles are alot of fun

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! I’m really glad you’re enjoying it. It’s awesome to hear the puzzles are hitting the right balance and that it even managed to get a scare out of you. Appreciate you giving it a try and taking the time to leave a review!

reviews.review_description: Very liminal space/backrooms vibe
